1.texto_original: Disse mais o Senhor a Moisés nas planícies de Moab, junto ao Jordão, na altura de Jericó:
1.normal:
Devotional Study of Números 35:1

"Disse mais o Senhor a Moisés nas planícies de Moab, junto ao Jordão, na altura de Jericó:"

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
1.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of Números 35:1

Philological Analysis

"Disse mais o Senhor a Moisés nas planícies de Moab, junto ao Jordão, na altura de Jericó:"

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
1.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"Disse mais o Senhor a Moisés nas planícies de Moab, junto ao Jordão, na altura de Jericó:"

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"Estas coisas vos serão por estatuto de direito pelas vossas gerações, em todos os lugares da vossa habitação."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

30.texto_original: Todo aquele que matar alguém, será morto conforme o depoimento de testemunhas; mas uma só testemunha não deporá contra alguém, para condená-lo à morte.
30.normal:
Devotional Study of Números 35:30

"Todo aquele que matar alguém, será morto conforme o depoimento de testemunhas; mas uma só testemunha não deporá contra alguém, para condená-lo à morte."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
30.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of Números 35:30

Philological Analysis

"Todo aquele que matar alguém, será morto conforme o depoimento de testemunhas; mas uma só testemunha não deporá contra alguém, para condená-lo à morte."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.

32.texto_original: Também não aceitareis resgate por aquele que se tiver acolhido à sua cidade de refúgio, a fim de que ele possa tornar a habitar na terra antes da morte do sumo sacerdote.

33.texto_original: Assim não profanareis a terra da vossa habitação, porque o sangue profana a terra; e nenhuma expiação se poderá fazer pela terra por causa do sangue que nela for derramado, senão com o sangue daquele que o derramou.
33.normal:
Devotional Study of Números 35:33

"Assim não profanareis a terra da vossa habitação, porque o sangue profana a terra; e nenhuma expiação se poderá fazer pela terra por causa do sangue que nela for derramado, senão com o sangue daquele que o derramou."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.

34.texto_original: Não contaminareis, pois, a terra em que haveis de habitar, no meio da qual eu também habitarei; pois eu, o Senhor, habito no meio dos filhos de Israel.
34.normal:
Devotional Study of Números 35:34

"Não contaminareis, pois, a terra em que haveis de habitar, no meio da qual eu também habitarei; pois eu, o Senhor, habito no meio dos filhos de Israel."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
